pattern: The day you first reach room i can be written purely in terms of the day you first reached room i-1 and the day you first reached whichever earlier room it loops back to, so the whole timeline collapses into one linear pass instead of a step-by-step simulation.
hints: Simulating day by day works for tiny cases but blows up for larger ones - the day count needed can grow exponentially with the number of rooms.
Let firstDay[i] be the day you first arrive at room i (firstDay[0] = 0). Reaching room i for the first time always means you just came from room i-1's second visit.

Derive the recurrence firstDay[i] = 2*firstDay[i-1] - firstDay[nextVisit[i-1]] + 2 (all taken modulo 1e9+7, watching out for a negative intermediate result before the mod). The answer is firstDay for the very last room.

edge_inputs.description: two rooms, a short bounce back
three rooms with a two-step loop back
four rooms chaining forward and looping to the start
minimum two rooms, second room loops to itself
every room loops all the way back to room zero
every room loops to its own index, no shortcuts
ten rooms all looping back to the very first room
a mix of near and far loop-backs

stress.description: large random room graphs near the max room count
